title: Clarify and enforce lifecycle field semantics across canonical responses
summary: Align the implementation and response contract for canonical lifecycle fields so `status`, `phase`, `next_action`, and terminality are internally consistent, machine-readable, and safe for AI-agent branching. This ticket focuses on removing ambiguity in how lifecycle state is represented after wrapper and helper operations, especially around the distinction between non-terminal `committed` and terminal `done` or `blocked`.
outputs: A clarified semantic contract for `canonical_status`, `canonical_phase`, `next_action`, and terminality across lifecycle-aware tool responses
Implementation changes that prevent contradictory or ambiguous lifecycle guidance from being returned
Regression tests that verify `done` and `blocked` remain terminal while `committed` remains non-terminal until explicit lifecycle closure
acceptance_criteria: The implementation defines and applies a consistent meaning for lifecycle `status`, `phase`, and `next_action` across lifecycle-aware responses.
Tool responses do not return contradictory combinations of lifecycle guidance fields.
`done` and `blocked` are always treated and reported as terminal states.
`committed` remains a non-terminal state until explicit terminal completion is recorded.
Responses after helper success accurately report whether lifecycle follow-up is still required.
Tests fail if future changes misreport terminality or return inconsistent `status` / `phase` / `next_action` combinations.
tasks: Audit lifecycle state-setting logic in wrapper and helper paths to identify inconsistent or ambiguous use of `status`, `phase`, and `next_action`.
Document the intended semantics for canonical lifecycle status, canonical workflow phase, next required lifecycle action, and terminal detection.
Update implementation paths so response builders expose a single internally consistent view of lifecycle state.
Verify that helper flows preserve the explicit distinction between non-terminal `committed` and terminal `done` or `blocked`.
Add regression tests that cover representative success and failure flows and assert correct terminal and non-terminal reporting.
notes: This ticket is not about redesigning the transaction model; it is about making the existing model easier for agents to consume correctly.
The most important semantic distinction to preserve is that helper completion may leave canonical state at non-terminal `committed`, with explicit follow-up still required.
This work should be implemented in terms of canonical state, not client-side inference or convenience shortcuts.
If any lifecycle field is derived from another in some contexts, that relationship should be made explicit in behavior and tests rather than left implicit.
